auction_style: Andrew Grossman is methodical and somewhat old-school — he's the one who still sends checks and references Strat-O-Matic. He's a die-hard Mets fan which historically colored his player preferences. The Widowmakers are a 2-time championship team (2010, 2019 co-champ) suggesting solid auction fundamentals. Andrew typically speaks for both himself and Susan on team decisions, and tends toward conservative, balanced roster construction.
price_tendency_detail: No direct price data. His trade behavior — asking for Lance Lynn in exchange for a quality OF — suggests he understands market value but may be conservative on closer/RP spending.
trade_style: Selectively active. The Grossmans will engage in trades when the deal is clearly explained. Andrew's response to the Gleyber/Baez megadeal was 'A Nimmo on the move would be a good thing' — he observes others' trades carefully and reacts. His own trade asking for Lance Lynn for an OF suggests he values SP-for-OF swaps. He confirmed Baez trade immediately which shows he's responsive.
notable_tendencies: Andrew is a die-hard Mets fan — historically may have had NY player bias at auction
Still sends paper checks to Lombardi — old-school approach extends to draft philosophy
Mets fan self-declared: 'I for one am a die-hard Mets fan and invite you to take that into consideration'
Signs emails as 'Baseball Widowmakers' or 'Da Widowmakers' — committed to league identity
2022: asked for Lance Lynn in exchange for non-Alvarez/non-Harper OF — values aces at premium
Congratulated Chathams: 'You made us dizzy with all of those moves' — not a high-churn team himself
intel_summary: The Grossmans are a steady, experienced team that competes without flash. Andrew's Mets fandom and old-school sensibilities suggest he may slightly overpay for likable/familiar players. He's not going to out-hustle you on waivers but he'll build a solid auction roster and hold firm in trade negotiations. Worth probing for SP trades mid-season — he seems to see pitching as a trade chip.
